name: arch_inv_vacuous_empty
code:
proof fn phi_1_arch_inv_vacuous_empty(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.layers.len() == 0,
    ensures
        arch.inv(),
{
}
reason: An arch with zero layers should not satisfy inv, as it represents no meaningful page-table structure

name: entry_base_collision
code:
proof fn phi_2_entry_base_collision(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i < arch.num_entries(layer),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, i) == arch.entry_base(layer, base, (i + 1) as nat),
{
}
reason: Consecutive entry bases being equal would mean distinct indices map overlapping address regions

name: dir_inv_wrong_page_size
code:
proof fn phi_3_dir_inv_wrong_page_size(dir: Directory, i: nat)
    requires
        dir.inv(),
        i < dir.entries.len(),
        dir.entries[i as int] is Page,
    ensures
        dir.entries[i as int]->Page_0.frame.size != dir.entry_size(),
{
}
reason: If a page frame size can differ from the layer's entry size under inv, address translation would be incorrect

name: well_formed_allows_empty
code:
proof fn phi_4_well_formed_allows_empty(dir: Directory)
    requires
        dir.well_formed(),
    ensures
        dir.entries.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: A well-formed directory with zero entries would make the page table level degenerate and break address space coverage

name: interp_page_mapping_lost
code:
proof fn phi_5_interp_page_mapping_lost(dir: Directory, j: nat, va: nat)
    requires
        dir.inv(),
        j < dir.entries.len(),
        dir.entries[j as int] is Page,
        dir.interp_of_entry(j).contains_key(va),
    ensures
        !dir.interp().contains_key(va),
{
}
reason: A page mapping present in an entry's interpretation but absent from the directory's full interpretation would silently drop valid mappings
